The A9 Trunk Road (A9 Helmsdale to Ord of Caithness Improvements Phase-2) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic, Temporary Prohibition of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restriction) Order 2007
The Prostitution (Public Places) (Scotland) Act 2007 (Commencement) Order 2007
items.description: This Order brings into force the Prostitution (Public Places) (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the Act”) on 15th October 2007.

items.title: The Nutrition and Health Claims (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, make provision to enforce Regulation (EC) No. 1924/2006 of the European Parliament and of the Council on nutrition and health claims made on foods, as corrected by a Corrigendum (O.J. No. L 12, 18.1.07, p.3) (“the EC Regulation”).

items.title: The A83 Trunk Road (Minard) (40mph Speed Limit) Variation and Minard Primary School (Part-time 20mph Speed Limit) Order 2007
The Protection of Vulnerable Groups (Scotland) Act 2007 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2007
items.description: This Order brings into force certain provisions of the Protection of Vulnerable Groups (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the Act”). Article 2(1) brings into force, on 1st September 2007, all the provisions of the Act listed in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Foot-and-Mouth Disease (Export Restrictions) (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations revoke and re-make with amendments the Import and Export Restrictions (Foot-and-Mouth Disease) (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 2007. They cease to have effect on 15th September 2007.

items.title: The Disease Control (Interim Measures) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2007
items.description: This Order amends the Disease Control (Interim Measures) (Scotland) Order 2002 (“the 2002 Order”).

items.title: The National Health Service (Charges for Drugs and Appliances) (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations provide for the making and recovery of charges for drugs and appliances (other than dental or optical appliances) supplied under or by virtue of the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Prohibition on the Removal of Shark Fins) (Scotland) Order 2007
items.description: This Order makes provision for the enforcement of Community restrictions and other obligations relating to the removal of shark fins on board vessels as set out in Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1185/2003 of 26th June 2003 (“the Regulation”).

items.title: The National Health Service (Pharmaceutical Services) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Pharmaceutical Services) (Scotland) Regulations 1995 (“the principal Regulations”) which regulate the provision of pharmaceutical services under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978.

items.title: The National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 2003 (“the Regulations”).

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical Services Contracts) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Medical Services Contracts) (Scotland) Regulations 2004 (“the principal Regulations”) which regulate contracts for the provision of general medical services under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978.

items.title: The National Health Service (Primary Medical Services Section 17C Agreements) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Primary Medical Services Section 17C Agreements) (Scotland) Regulations 2004 (“the principal Regulations”) which regulate the provision of primary medical services under agreements under section 17C of the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978.

items.title: The Gambling Act 2005 (Review of Premises Licences) (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations make provision about:

items.title: The Gambling Act 2005 (Fees No. 3) (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations prescribe certain fees in relation to the registration of non-commercial societies for the promotion of small society lotteries under the Gambling Act 2005. Regulation 2 sets an application fee of £40. Regulation 3 provides for an annual fee of £20 to be payable by a registered society to the registering licensing board.

items.title: The Provision of School Education for Children under School Age (Prescribed Children) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2007
items.description: Section 32 of the Standards in Scotland’s Schools etc. Act 2000 amended section 1 of the Education (Scotland) Act 1980 (“the 1980 Act”) by imposing a duty on an education authority to secure the provision of school education for categories of children as may be prescribed by the Scottish Ministers. Section 1(1B) of the 1980 Act provides the power to prescribe the amount of school education that children within those categories are to receive.

items.title: The Licensing (Training of Staff) (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: In terms of paragraph 6 of schedule 3 to the Licensing (Scotland) Act 2005, no person (other than a personal licence holder) is to sell or serve alcohol on licensed premises unless that person has complied with any training requirements prescribed by regulations. These Regulations provide that any person working in such a capacity must have received at least 2 hours' training from a personal licence holder or a person holding a qualification mentioned in regulation 2(b). The training must cover the matters specified in the Schedule to the Regulations.

items.title: The Private Security Industry Act 2001 (Designated Activities) (Scotland) Order 2007
items.description: This Order designates certain activities of security operatives for the purposes of section 3 of the Private Security Industry Act 2001 (“the 2001 Act”), which are activities that may only be undertaken under and in accordance with a licence granted by the Security Industry Authority.

items.title: The Water Industry Commissioner for Scotland (Dissolution) Order 2007
items.description: This Order appoints 1st October 2007 as the date for the dissolution of the office of the Water Industry Commissioner for Scotland (“the Commissioner”). The functions of the Commissioner are now carried out by the Water Industry Commission for Scotland, a body corporate established under section 1 of the Water Services etc. (Scotland) Act 2005 (asp 3).

items.title: The Road Works (Inspection Fees) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations provide for an increase to £24.00 (previously £21.00) in the fee payable by undertakers for inspections of their work by road works authorities in Scotland under regulation 3(1) of the Road Works (Inspection Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 2003 and revokes a previous instrument which increased the fees in 2004.

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Restriction on Days at Sea) (Scotland) Order 2007
items.description: This Order provides for the enforcement in Scotland of the provisions of Annex IIa and IIc (“the Annexes”) to Council Regulation 41/2006 fixing for certain fish stocks and groups of fish stocks the fishing opportunities in Community waters for 2007 and the monitoring, inspection and surveillance provisions in Council Regulation 423/2004.

items.title: The Disease Control (Interim Measures) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Order 2007
items.description: This Order amends the Disease Control (Interim Measures) (Scotland) Order 2002 (“the 2002 Order”).

items.title: The A75 Trunk Road (Cairntop to Barlae Dual Carriageway) Order 2007
The A75 Trunk Road (Cairntop to Barlae Dual Carriageway) (Side Roads) Order 2007
The A68 Trunk Road (Dalkeith Northern Bypass) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic, Temporary Prohibition of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restriction) Order 2007
The South West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 8) Order 2007
The South East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 8) Order 2007
The North East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 8) Order 2007
The Regulation of Care (Social Service Workers) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2007
items.description: Section 44(1) of the Regulation of Care (Scotland) Act 2001 (“the Act”) requires the Scottish Social Services Council to maintain a register of social workers, social service workers of any other description prescribed and persons participating in an approved course or employed in a position probationary to becoming either a social worker or a social service worker of such other description.

items.title: The North West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 8) Order 2007
The Transport (Scotland) Act 2005 (Commencement No. 3) Order 2007
items.description: This Order, at article 2, appoints 1st October 2007 as the day for the coming into force of sections 25 (enforcement of sections 118 and 119 of the 1991 Act) and section 39 (consultation on regulations and codes of practice) of the Transport (Scotland) Act 2005 (“the Act”). Section 25 inserts a new section 119A into the New Roads and Street Works Act 1991. Section 39 inserts a new section 163A into that Act.

items.title: The A90 Trunk Road (Foveran)(50mph Speed Limit) Variation and Foveran Primary School (Part-time 20mph Speed Limit) Order 2007
The Plant Protection Products (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Plant Protection Products (Scotland) Regulations 2005 (“the principal Regulations”) which implement in Scotland Council Directive 91/414/EEC concerning the placing of plant protection products on the market (“the Directive”).

items.title: The Scottish Road Works Commissioner (Imposition of Penalties) Regulations 2007
items.description: Section 119A of the New Roads and Street Works Act 1991 (inserted by section 25 of the Transport (Scotland) Act 2005) allows the Scottish Ministers, by regulations, to make provision for or in connection with the imposition by the Scottish Road Works Commissioner of penalties on road works authorities and undertakers who fail to comply with the duties imposed on them by sections 118 and 119 of the 1991 Act respectively. Section 118 relates to the general duty of road works authority to co-ordinate works and section 119 relates to the general duty of undertakers to co-operate. These Regulations exercise that power.

items.title: The Miscellaneous Food Additives and the Sweeteners in Food Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, amend the Miscellaneous Food Additives Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/3187 as amended) (“the Additives Regulations”) in order to provide for the implementation of–

items.title: The National Health Service (Primary Medical Services Performers Lists) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend The National Health Service (Primary Medical Services Performers Lists) (Scotland) Regulations 2004 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Common Agricultural Policy Single Farm Payment and Support Schemes (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Common Agricultural Policy Single Farm Payment and Support Schemes (Scotland) Regulations 2005 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Plant Health (Scotland) (Amendment) Order 2007 (revoked)
items.description: This Order amends Schedules 16 and 17 to the Plant Health (Scotland) Order 2005 (“the principal Order”). The Schedules, which implement Council Directive 93/85/EEC and Council Directive 98/57/EC contain special measures on the control of Potato Ring Rot and Ralstonia solanacearum respectively.

items.title: The Plant Health (Phytophthora ramorum) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2007
items.description: This Order amends the Plant Health (Phytophthora ramorum) (Scotland) Order 2004 (S.S.I. 2004/488) (“the Order”) so as to implement Commission Decision

items.title: The Disclosure Scotland (Staff Transfer) Order 2007
items.description: This Order provides for certain employees of the Strathclyde Joint Police Board (“the Police Board”) to transfer to, and become members of the staff of, the Scottish Administration (meaning the staff of the Scottish Executive and of certain non ministerial office holders). The staff who will transfer are those who work in Disclosure Scotland discharging Scottish Ministers' functions relating to criminal records certificates in terms of Part 5 of the Police Act 1997 (c. 50). The staff are currently employed as civilian (non constable) employees of the Police Board under section 9 of the Police (Scotland) Act 1967 (c. 77). The transfer of the staff takes place on 1st October 2007. The staff are identified in the Schedule (introduced by article 2) according to their employee references. One hundred and one employees of the Police Board will be transferred to the staff of the Scottish Administration.

items.title: The Seed Potatoes (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, amend the Seed Potatoes (Scotland) Regulations 2000 (S.S.I. 2000/201) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Housing (Scotland) Act 2006 (Repayment Charge and Discharge) Order 2007
items.description: The Schedules to this Order set out the form of a repayment charge and the form of a discharge of a repayment charge which can be made by a local authority under Part 7 of the Housing (Scotland) Act 2006.

items.title: The Scottish Local Government Elections Order 2007
items.description: This Order, which revokes and replaces the Scottish Local Elections Rules 2002 as amended (“the 2002 Rules”), provides for the conduct of elections of members of local authorities.

items.title: The Zoonoses (Monitoring) (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations make provision in Scotland for the administration and enforcement of Directive 2003/99/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on the monitoring of zoonoses and zoonotic agents, amending Council Decision 90/424/EEC and repealing Council Directive 92/117/EEC.

items.title: The Porcine Semen (Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the payment of fees in connection with the issue of licences or approvals under the Artificial Insemination of Pigs (Scotland) Regulations 1964 and the Artificial Insemination of Pigs (EEC) Regulations 1992. They also make provision for the payment of fees in connection with tests or examinations carried out for the purposes of those Regulations.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Dental Services) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Dental Services) (Scotland) Regulations 1996 (“the Regulations”), which make provisions as to the arrangements under which dentists provide general dental services under the National Health Service in Scotland.

items.title: The Disease Control (Interim Measures) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 3) Order 2007
items.description: This Order amends the Disease Control (Interim Measures) (Scotland) Order 2002 (“the 2002 Order”).

items.title: The Food for Particular Nutritional Uses (Scotland) (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, implement a number of Community instruments relating to foods for particular nutritional uses through amendment of a series of statutory instruments.

items.title: The Civil Legal Aid (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend the Civil Legal Aid (Scotland) Regulations 2002 to provide for legal aid subject to modified means tests in respect of various guardianship order applications made under the Adults with Incapacity(Scotland) Act 2000.

items.title: The University of the West of Scotland Order of Council 2007
items.description: This Order makes amendments to the governance instruments of the University of Paisley consequential on its change of name to the University of the West of Scotland.

items.title: The Surface Waters (Shellfish) (Classification) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, amend the Surface Waters (Shellfish) (Classification) (Scotland) Regulations 1997 (“the 1997 Regulations”).

items.title: The Import and Export Restrictions (Foot-and-Mouth Disease) (Scotland) (No. 3) Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations revoke and re-make with amendments the Foot-and-Mouth Disease (Export Restrictions) (Scotland) Regulations 2007.

items.title: The Foot-and-Mouth Disease (Scotland) Amendment Order 2007
items.description: This Order amends the Foot-and-Mouth Disease (Scotland) Order 2006.

items.title: The South West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.1) Order 2007
The A82 Trunk Road (Strowanswell Road, Dumbarton) (Temporary Prohibition of Specified Turns) Order 2007
The Custodial Sentences and Weapons (Scotland) Act 2007 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2007
items.description: This Commencement Order brings into force sections 58, 60(1)(a) and (b), 61 to 63 and 66(2) and schedule 5 to the Custodial Sentences and Weapons (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the 2007 Act”). Sections 58, 60(1)(b), 66(2) and schedule 5 are only brought into force for certain specified purposes.

items.title: The Shetland Islands Council (Uyea Sound, Unst) Harbour Jurisdiction Order 2007
items.description: Shetland Islands Council exercise jurisdiction as harbour authority under the Zetland County Council Act 1974 in respect of the area specified in Schedule 1 to that Act.

items.title: The Plastic Materials and Articles in Contact with Food (Lid Gaskets) (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, provide for the execution and enforcement of Commission Regulation (EC) No. 372/2007 laying down transitional migration limits for plasticisers in gaskets in lids intended to come into contact with foods (“the Commission Regulation”).

items.title: The Police and Justice Act 2006 (Commencement) (Scotland) Order 2007
items.description: This Order, which extends only to Scotland, brings into force provisions in the Police and Justice Act 2006 (“the 2006 Act”) relating to computer misuse on 1st October 2007.

items.title: The Natural Mineral Water, Spring Water and Bottled Drinking Water (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, revoke and re-enact with changes the Natural Mineral Water, Spring Water and Bottled Drinking Water Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/1540) as last amended by the Natural Mineral Water, Spring Water and Bottled Drinking Water Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2004 S.I. 2004/132.

items.title: The Administrative Justice and Tribunals Council (Listed Tribunals) (Scotland) Order 2007
items.description: This Order is made under the Tribunals, Courts and Enforcement Act 2007 (“the 2007 Act”). The 2007 Act establishes the Administrative Justice and Tribunals Council (“the AJTC”), which has both a Scottish and a Welsh Committee, to replace the Council on Tribunals and its Scottish Committee. It provides for the AJTC to keep under review, consider and report on matters relating to “listed tribunals”.

items.title: The Smoking, Health and Social Care (Scotland) Act 2005 (Variation of Age Limit for Sale of Tobacco etc. and Consequential Modifications) Order 2007
items.description: By virtue of section 9(1) of the Smoking, Health and Social Care (Scotland) Act 2005 (“the 2005 Act”) the Scottish Ministers may, by order, modify section 18 of the Children and Young Persons (Scotland) Act 1937 (offence of selling tobacco etc. to under age persons and other preventative measures) (“the 1937 Act”) so as to substitute for the age specified in any of its provisions (at the making of this Order, 16) such other higher age or ages as they consider appropriate. Section 39 of the 2005 Act enables the Scottish Ministers, by order, to make such incidental, supplemental or consequential provision as they consider necessary or expedient for the purposes, or in consequence, of that Act. Any order under section 39 may make different provision for different purposes, and may modify any enactment, instrument or document.

items.title: The Civil Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend the Civil Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees) Regulations 19889 to allow increased fees where in civil cases following the coming into force of the Vulnerable Witnesses (Scotland) Act 2004 an assisted person or a witness is a vulnerable witness (articles 4 and 6).

items.title: The Less Favoured Area Support Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations, which replace the Less Favoured Area Support Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2005 (“the 2005 Regulations”), make provision for the purposes of implementation of Council Regulation (EC) No. 1698/2005 on support for rural development from the European Agricultural Fund for Rural Development and Commission Regulations (EC) Nos. 1974/2006 and 1975/2006 laying down detailed rules for the application of Council Regulation No. 1698/2005. Regulation 1689/2005 will preserve, until 2010, elements of Regulation (EC) 1257/1999 which apply to Less Favoured Area Support.

items.title: The South East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.1) Order 2007
Act of Sederunt (Sheriff Court Ordinary Cause, Summary Application, Summary Cause and Small Claim Rules) Amendment (Council Regulation (EC) No. 1348 of 2000 Extension to Denmark) 2007
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes various amendments to the rules applicable in the sheriff court relating to the service of documents on persons outwith Scotland. Those amendments are consequential upon the extension of Council Regulation (EC) No. 1348/2000 of 29th May 2000 on service in the Member States of judicial and extrajudicial documents in civil or commercial matters to the Kingdom of Denmark by an Agreement between the European Community and the Kingdom of Denmark dated 19th October 2005 and coming into force on 1st July 2007.

items.title: The A77 Trunk Road (Haggstone Climbing Lane and Glen App Carriageway Improvement) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic, Temporary Prohibition of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restriction) Order 2007
The South West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 9) Order 2007
The South East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 9) Order 2007
The North West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 9) Order 2007
The North East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 9) Order 2007
The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (City of Edinburgh) Designation Amendment Order 2007
items.description: This Order amends the Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (City of Edinburgh) Designation Order 1998.

items.title: The Vulnerable Witnesses (Scotland) Act 2004 (Commencement No. 6, Savings and Transitional Provisions) Order 2007
items.description: This Commencement Order brings into force sections 11 to 17, section 18(1)(b) to (e) and (2) and sections 20 to 22 of the Vulnerable Witnesses (Scotland) Act 2004 (“the 2004 Act”) insofar as not already in force. It also brings into force sections 18(1)(a) and 19 of the 2004 Act, insofar as not already in force, for the purpose indicated in the Schedule to this Order.

items.title: The Scottish Commission for Human Rights Act 2006 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2007
items.description: This Order brings into force on 8th November 2007 various provisions of the Scottish Commission for Human Rights Act 2006 (“the Act”).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No. 8) (Miscellaneous) 2007
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes miscellaneous amendments to the Rules of the Court of Session 1994 (S.I. 1994/1443).

items.title: The North West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 1) Order 2007
Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No. 9) (Vulnerable Witnesses (Scotland) Act 2004) 2007
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes amendments to the Rules of the Court of Session 1994 (S.I. 1994/1443). These amendments are a consequence of the implementation of the Vulnerable Witnesses (Scotland) Act 2004 (asp 3) to civil proceedings. A new Chapter 35A is inserted to provide procedure and forms for dealing with child witness notices, vulnerable witness applications and applications to review orders specifying the special measures to be used when taking evidence from a vulnerable witness. It also sets out the procedure for the special measure of taking evidence by a commissioner. The witness citation form is also amended to give a witness some information about the possibility of giving evidence with a special measure if they are a vulnerable witness.

items.title: The Provision of School Lunches (Disapplication of the Requirement to Charge) (Scotland) Order 2007
items.description: Section 57 of the Local Government in Scotland Act 2003 (“the 2003 Act”) provides, among other things, for the Scottish Ministers by order to amend, repeal, revoke or disapply any legislation that they consider prevents local authorities from exercising their power under section 20(1) of the 2003 Act to do anything which the authority considers is likely to promote or improve the well-being of its area, people in that area or both of these. The section 20(1) power is known as the local authorities' ‘power to advance well-being’. The powers of the Scottish Ministers under section 57 may be exercised in relation to all local authorities, a class or classes of local authority, or a particular local authority or local authorities. The legislation concerned may be amended or disapplied for a particular period of time. This is the first order made under section 57 of the 2003 Act.

items.title: The Premises Licence (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations make provision in connection with premises licences under Part 3 of the Licensing (Scotland) Act 2005 (“the Act”). The form of a premises licence and of a summary of such a licence are set by regulations 2 and 3 and Schedules 1 and 2. Regulation 4 and Schedules 3 and 4 set down the form of an application for a premises licence. In terms of section 20(2) of the Act, any application for a premises licence requires to be accompanied by a layout plan and an operating plan. Regulation 5 makes provision as to the layout plan and regulation 6 and Schedule 5 prescribe the form of an operating plan.

items.title: The Licensing (Procedure) (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations make provision as to various procedural matters under the Licensing (Scotland) Act 2005 (“the Act”). The Act will be fully operational with effect from 1st September 2009. These Regulations come into force on 1st February 2008, the date as from which it will be possible to lodge a licence application under the Act.

items.title: The Licensing (Transitional and Saving Provisions) (Scotland) Order 2007
items.description: The new regime for licensing the sale of alcohol under the Licensing (Scotland) Act 2005 (“the 2005 Act”) will be fully operational with effect from 1st September 2009. This Order makes transitional and saving provision in connection with the move to that regime from the existing one under the Licensing (Scotland) Act 1976 (“the 1976 Act”). Article 2 defines “the transitional period” as the period from 1st February 2008 until 5.00am on 1st September 2009.

items.title: The Foot-and-Mouth Disease (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Order 2007
items.description: This Order amends the Foot-and-Mouth Disease (Scotland) Order 2006 (“the principal Order”).

items.title: The Housing Grants (Assessment of Contributions) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations make amendments consequential upon the coming into force of section 61 of the Welfare Reform Act 2007 (c. 5). Section 61 amends the Disability (Grants) Act 1993 to enable the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions to make grants to the Independent Living Fund (2006) which from 1st October 2007 will operate in place of the Independent Living (Extension) Fund and the Independent Living (1993) Fund.

items.title: The Licensing (Mandatory Conditions) (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: Schedule 3 to the Licensing (Scotland) Act 2005 lists mandatory conditions which must be imposed by a Licensing Board on the granting of a premises licence. These Regulations amend that schedule by adding two further mandatory conditions. The new conditions relate to notices on the admission of under-18s and the provision of baby changing facilities.

items.title: The Community Care (Direct Payments) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007
items.description: Section 12B of the Social Work (Scotland) Act 1968 requires a local authority to make direct payments to persons in respect of securing the provision of certain services, where that person consents, or consent is given on their behalf.

items.title: The A68 Trunk Road (Dalkeith Northern Bypass) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic, Temporary Prohibition of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restriction) Order 2007
The North East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.1) Order 2007
The Import and Export Restrictions (Foot-and-Mouth Disease) (Scotland) (No. 4) Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations revoke and re-make with amendments the Import and Export Restrictions (Foot-and-Mouth Disease) (Scotland) (No. 3) Regulations 2007.

items.title: The Gambling Act 2005 (Fees No. 4) (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: Part 9 of the Gambling Act 2005 (“the Act”) enables a person holding an operating licence to give a temporary use notice in respect of premises. The effect of the notice is to authorise the premises to be used during the period specified in the notice for carrying on gambling activities without an offence being committed under section 37 of the Act (which makes it unlawful to use premises for providing facilities for gambling without the appropriate premises licence). These Regulations provide that a fee of £350 will be payable on giving a temporary use notice in relation to premises in Scotland.

items.title: The A985 Trunk Road (Limekilns) (50mph Speed Limit) Order 2007
Act of Sederunt (Ordinary Cause, Summary Application, Summary Cause and Small Claim Rules) Amendment (Vulnerable Witnesses (Scotland) Act 2004) 2007
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes miscellaneous amendments to the rules of procedure in the sheriff court as follows.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Sheriff Court Company Insolvency Rules 1986) Amendment (Vulnerable Witnesses (Scotland) Act 2004) 2007
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Sheriff Court Company Insolvency Rules 1986. It gives the sheriff the same powers to deal with applications in relation to vulnerable witnesses and the special measures that may be ordered in relation to vulnerable witnesses within the meaning of the Vulnerable Witnesses (Scotland) Act 2004 (“the 2004 Act”) that are held by the sheriff under Chapter 45 of the Ordinary Cause Rules in the First Schedule to the Sheriff Courts (Scotland) Act 1907.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Proceedings in the Sheriff Court under the Debtors (Scotland) Act 1987) Amendment (Vulnerable Witnesses (Scotland) Act 2004) 2007
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the rules of procedure in the sheriff court for proceedings under the Debtors (Scotland) Act 1987. It extends the powers of the sheriff under the rules to include the powers held by the sheriff under Chapter 45 of the Ordinary Cause Rules in the First Schedule of the Sheriff Courts (Scotland) Act 1907 in dealing with vulnerable witnesses within the meaning of the Vulnerable Witnesses (Scotland) Act 2004 (“the 2004 Act”).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Debt Arrangement and Attachment (Scotland) Act 2002) Amendment (Vulnerable Witnesses (Scotland) Act 2004) 2007
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the rules of procedure for applications in the sheriff court under the Debt Arrangement and Attachment (Scotland) Act 2002. It gives the sheriff the same powers to deal with applications in relation to vulnerable witnesses and the special measures that may be ordered in relation to vulnerable witnesses within the meaning of the Vulnerable Witnesses (Scotland) Act 2004 (“the 2004 Act”) that are held by the sheriff under Chapter 45 of the Ordinary Cause Rules in the First Schedule of the Sheriff Courts (Scotland) Act 1907.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Sheriff Court Bankruptcy Rules 1996) Amendment (Vulnerable Witnesses (Scotland) Act 2004) 2007
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Sheriff Court Bankruptcy Rules 1996. It gives the sheriff the same powers to deal with applications in relation to vulnerable witnesses and the special measures that may be ordered in relation to vulnerable witnesses within the meaning of the Vulnerable Witnesses (Scotland) Act 2004 (“the 2004 Act”) that are held by the sheriff under Chapter 45 of the Ordinary Cause Rules in the First Schedule to the Sheriff Courts (Scotland) Act 1907.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Child Care and Maintenance Rules 1997) Amendment (Vulnerable Witnesses (Scotland) Act 2004) 2007
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Act of Sederunt (Child Care and Maintenance Rules) 1997 (“the 1997 Rules”) to give the sheriff the same powers to deal with applications in relation to vulnerable witnesses and the special measures that may be ordered in relation to vulnerable witnesses within the meaning of the Vulnerable Witnesses (Scotland) Act 2004 (“the 2004 Act”) that are held by the sheriff under Chapter 45 of the Ordinary Cause Rules in the First Schedule to the Sheriff Courts (Scotland) Act 1907. These powers apply to all hearings under the 1997 Rules, except those hearings where rules in relation to vulnerable witnesses already apply under Part XI of Chapter 3 of the 1997 Rules.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Chancery Procedure Rules 2006) Amendment (Vulnerable Witnesses (Scotland) Act 2004) 2007
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Chancery Procedure Rules 2006. It gives the sheriff the same powers to deal with applications in relation to vulnerable witnesses, and the special measures that may be ordered in relation to vulnerable witnesses within the meaning of the Vulnerable Witnesses (Scotland) Act 2004 (“the 2004 Act”), that are held by the sheriff under Chapter 45 of the Ordinary Cause Rules in the First Schedule to the Sheriff Courts (Scotland) Act 1907.

items.title: The Tourist Boards (Scotland) Act 2006 (Commencement) Order 2007
items.description: This Order appoints 1st April 2007 as the day on which the Tourist Boards (Scotland) Act 2006, except section 5, will come into force. Section 5 relates to commencement and short title and came into force on Royal Assent (30th November 2006).

items.title: The Contaminants in Food (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend the Contaminants in Food (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (S.S.I. 2007/29) (“the 2007 Regulations”). They make provision for the execution and enforcement of Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1126/2007 amending Regulation (EC) No. 1881/2006 setting maximum levels for certain contaminants in foodstuffs as regards Fusarium toxins in maize and maize products.

items.title: The Materials and Articles in Contact with Food (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Materials and Articles in Contact with Food (Scotland) Regulations (S.S.I. 2005/243) (“the 2005 Regulations”) and re enact or re enact with amendments provisions contained in those Regulations. These Regulations provide for the enforcement of Regulation (EC) No. 1935/2004 of the European Parliament and of the Council on materials and articles intended to come into contact with food and repealing Directives 80/590/EEC and 89/109/EEC (O.J. No. L 338, 13.11.04, p.4) (“Regulation 1935/2004”).

items.title: The Licensing (Scotland) Act 2005 (Commencement No. 4) Order 2007
items.description: This Order brings certain specified provisions of the Licensing (Scotland) Act 2005 into force on 1st February 2008 (article 2 and Schedules 1 and 2). So far as not already in force, that Act is brought into force at 5.00 a.m. on 1st September 2009 (article 3).

items.title: The Import and Export Restrictions (Foot-and-Mouth Disease) (Scotland) (No. 5) Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations revoke and re-make with amendments the Import and Export Restrictions (Foot-and-Mouth Disease) (Scotland) (No. 4) Regulations 2007.

items.title: The A83 Trunk Road (Lochgilphead) (Part-Time 20mph Speed Limit And 30mph Speed Limit) Order 2007
The Housing (Scotland) Act 2006 (Consequential Amendments) Order 2007
items.description: This Order makes amendments to 5 statutory instruments to replace references to “rent assessment panel” and “rent assessment committee” with “private rented housing panel” and “private rented housing committee” respectively. The amendments are consequential upon section 21 of the Housing (Scotland) Act 2006 (asp 1), which makes provision for the names of the panel and committee.

items.title: The A876 Trunk Road (Upper Forth Crossing at Kincardine) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic, Temporary Prohibition of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restriction) (No. 2) Order 2007
The M876 Special Road (Upper Forth Crossing at Kincardine) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic, Temporary Prohibition of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restriction) (No. 2) Order 2007
The Fatal Accidents and Sudden Deaths Inquiry Procedure (Scotland) Amendment Rules 2007
items.description: These Rules amend the Fatal Accidents and Sudden Deaths Inquiry Procedure (Scotland) Rules 1977 (“the principal Rules”) on the conduct of fatal accident inquiries (“FAIs”).

items.title: The Criminal Proceedings etc. (Reform) (Scotland) Act 2007 (Commencement No. 2 and Transitional Provisions and Savings) Order 2007
items.description: The Criminal Proceedings etc. (Reform) (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the 2007 Act”) received Royal Assent on 22nd February 2007.

items.title: The A78 Trunk Road (Wemyss Bay) (30mph and 40mph Speed Limit) Order 2007
The District Courts and Justices of the Peace (Scotland) Order 2007
items.description: This Order is made under powers contained in the Criminal Proceedings etc. (Reform) (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the 2007 Act”).

items.title: The Pesticides (Maximum Residue Levels in Crops, Food and Feeding Stuffs) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which are made under section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972, amend the Pesticides (Maximum Residue Levels in Crops, Food and Feeding Stuffs) (Scotland) Regulations 2005 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Perth Harbour Revision Order 2007
items.description: This Order extends the limits of the harbour of Perth, within which the Perth and Kinross Council exercise jurisdiction as harbour and pilotage authority. The new harbour limits are described in article 3 of the Order.

items.title: The Natural Mineral Water, Spring Water and Bottled Drinking Water (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, revoke and re-enact with changes the Natural Mineral Water, Spring Water and Bottled Drinking Water Regulations 1999 (S.I. 1999/1540) as last amended by the Natural Mineral Water, Spring Water and Bottled Drinking Water Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2004 S.I. 2004/132.

items.title: The Environmental Impact Assessment (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations are made under section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972. They amend the Environmental Impact Assessment (Scotland) Regulations 1999 (“the 1999 Regulations”). The Regulations implement, in Scotland, Council Directive 85/337/EEC on the assessment of the effects of certain public and private projects on the environment (O.J. No. L 175, 5.7.1985, p.40), as amended by Council Directive 97/11/EC (O.J. No. L 73, 14.3.1997, p.5) and Council Directive 2003/35/EC (O.J. L 156, 25.6.03, p.17) in relation to applications for the approval of reserved matters made in connection with the grant of outline planning permission.

items.title: The Environmental Impact Assessment and Natural Habitats (Extraction of Minerals by Marine Dredging) (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement, in waters in Scotland and the Scottish zone referred to in the Regulations as  “marine waters”:

items.title: The M8/A8 Trunk Road (Chapleton Street to Anderson Street, Port Glasgow) (Temporary 40mph Speed Limit) (No2) Order 2007
The Education (School and Placing Information) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations are made under sections 2, 28B(1)(a)(ii) and (3) of the Education (Scotland) Act 1980 (“the  1980 Act”). The Education (School and Placing Information) (Scotland) Regulations 1982 (“the 1982 Regulations”) relate to the supply and availability of information as to schools and arrangements with respect to placing requests for places in schools under the management of an education authority. These Regulations make further amendments to the 1982 Regulations by amending regulations providing for Records of Needs, placing requests and School Boards.

items.title: The North West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 10) Order 2007
The South East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 10) Order 2007
The Planning etc. (Scotland) Act 2006 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2007
items.description: This Order brings into force on 6th February 2007 section 3 and Part 9 of the Planning etc. (Scotland) Act 2006 (“the Act”) for the limited purposes of enabling the Scottish Ministers to make, or to make provision by means of, subordinate legislation.

items.title: The North East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 10) Order 2007
The South West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 10) Order 2007
The Feed (Specified Undesirable Substances) (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations make further amendments to the Feeding Stuffs (Scotland) Regulations 2005 (“the Feeding Stuffs Regulations”).

items.title: The Feed (Corn Gluten Feed and Brewers Grains) (Emergency Control) (Scotland) Revocation Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Feed (Corn Gluten Feed and Brewers Grains) (Emergency Control) (Scotland) Regulations 2005 and make consequential amendments to The Genetically Modified Animal Feed (Scotland) Regulations and the Official Feed and Food Controls (Scotland) Regulations 2005 (S.S.I. 2005/616).

items.title: The Import and Export Restrictions (Foot-and-Mouth Disease) (Scotland) (No. 6) Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations revoke and re-make with amendments the Import and Export Restrictions (Foot-and-Mouth Disease) (Scotland) (No. 5) Regulations 2007.

items.title: Act of Adjournal (Criminal Procedure Rules Amendment No. 5) (Miscellaneous) 2007
items.description: This Act of Adjournal makes miscellaneous amendments to the Criminal Procedure Rules 1996 (S.I. 1996/513) (“the Rules”).

items.title: The Small Claims (Scotland) Amendment Order 2007
items.description: This Order amends the Small Claims (Scotland) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/1999) (“the 1988 Order”). Under the 1988 Order, small claims cannot exceed £750 in amount (exclusive of interest and expenses). The Order raises the limit of £750 to £3000. Actions for personal injury are excluded from the category of actions which must be brought as a small claim. The Order also amends the amount of expenses which the sheriff may award in a small claim where the value of the claim exceeds £200. The sheriff may now, where the value of the claim is £1500 or less, award expenses up to £150. Where the value of the claim is greater than £1500, the sheriff may award expenses up to 10% of the value of the claim (article 2).

items.title: The Legal Profession and Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 2007 (Commencement No. 4) Order 2007
items.description: This Order brings into force on 23rd November 2007 various provision of the Legal Profession and Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Plant Health (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Order 2007 (revoked)
items.description: This Order amends the Plant Health (Scotland) Order 2005 (S.S.I. 2005/613) (“the principal Order”) so as to implement the instruments below–

items.title: The Plant Health (Import Inspection Fees) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Plant Health (Import Inspection Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 2005 (“the principal Regulations”). The principal Regulations implement Article 13d of Council Directive 2000/29/EC (O.J. No. L 169, 10.7.00,p.1) which requires Member States to charge fees to cover the costs occasioned by the documentary checks, identity checks and plant health checks of certain imports of plants, plant products and other items from third countries.

items.title: The Tarbert (Loch Fyne) Harbour Revision (Constitution) Order 2007
items.description: This Order reconstitutes the Tarbert (Loch Fyne) Harbour Trustees as from 1st April 2008. It provides for the Trustees to consist of a body of eight Trustees with experience in relevant matters. Four Trustees will be elected by the Tarbert electors and three Trustees will be appointed by the Trustees. The senior port manager will also be a Trustee.

items.title: The Water Environment and Water Services (Scotland) Act 2003 (Commencement No. 5) Order 2007
items.description: This Order brings into force certain provisions of section 24 of the Water Environment and Water Services (Scotland) Act 2003 on 6th February 2007 for the purpose of enabling provisions to be made by order and for all remaining purposes on 31st March 2007. Section 24 amends the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1997 (“the 1997 Act”) to introduce planning controls over marine fish farming in transitional and coastal water. Subsection (2)(a) inserts new definitions into section 26 of the 1997 Act to extend the meaning of development to certain works in marine waters. Subsection 2(b) introduces two new subsections (6A) and (6B) into section 26 of the 1997 Act. These provisions confer powers on the Scottish Ministers to make orders making modifications (including amendments or repeals) as they consider necessary or expedient in respect of the definitions of “fish farming” and “equipment” in subsection (6) of section 26.

items.title: The National Health Service (Pharmaceutical Services) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Pharmaceutical Services) (Scotland) Regulations 1995 (“the principal regulations”) which regulate the provision of pharmaceutical services under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical Services Contracts) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Medical Services Contracts) (Scotland) Regulations 2004 (“the principal Regulations”) which regulate contracts for the provision of general medical services under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978.

items.title: The National Health Service (Primary Medical Services Section 17C Agreements) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Primary Medical Services Section 17C Agreements) (Scotland) Regulations 2004 (“the principal Regulations”) which regulate the provision of primary medical services under agreements under section 17C of the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978.

items.title: The Education (Amendments in respect of Graduate Endowment, Student Fees and Support) (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Fees and Awards) (Scotland) Regulations 2007, the Students' Allowances (Scotland) Regulations 2007, the Education Maintenance Allowances (Scotland) Regulations 2007, the Graduate Endowment (Scotland) Regulations 2007, the Education Authority Bursaries (Scotland) Regulations 2007, the Nursing and Midwifery Student Allowances (Scotland) Regulations 2007, the Education (Student Loans) (Scotland) Regulations 2007 and the Education (Student Loans for Tuition Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 2006.

items.title: The Club Gaming and Club Machine Permits (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: Sections 271 and 273 of the Gambling Act 2005 make provision for club gaming permits to be issued to members' clubs and miners' welfare institutes and for club machine permits to be issued to such clubs and institutes or to commercial clubs. In Scotland, the issuing authority is the Licensing Board for the area in which the premises in question are situated.

items.title: The Licensed Premises Gaming Machine Permits (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations make provision in relation to a licensed premises gaming machine permit where the applicant for or holder of the permit holds a licence granted under section 9(1) of the Licensing (Scotland) Act 1976 (licence for sale by retail or supply of alcoholic liquor) provided it is not an off-sale licence.

items.title: The Bee Diseases and Pests Control (Scotland) Order 2007
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the Bee Diseases Control Order 1982 (S.I. 1982/107) and revokes the Importation of Bees Order 1997 (S.I. 1997/310) in so far as those instruments relate to Scotland.

items.title: The Sheriff Courts (Scotland) Act 1971 (Privative Jurisdiction and Summary Cause) Order 2007
items.description: This Order raises the limit of the privative (or exclusive) jurisdiction of the sheriff court and the limit of the summary cause from £1500 to £5000. The new limits do not apply to proceedings commenced before the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Remote Monitoring Requirements (Prescribed Courts) (Scotland) Revocation Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Remote Monitoring Requirements (Prescribed Courts) (Scotland) Regulations 2005 (“the 2005 Regulations”). Those Regulations prescribed courts, and descriptions of courts, for the purposes of section 24A(1) and (2) of the Criminal Procedure (Scotland) Act 1995 (power to grant bail subject to a remote monitoring requirement).

items.title: The A9 Trunk Road (Helmsdale to Ord of Caithness Improvements - Phase 2) (Side Roads) (No. 2) Order 2007
The A85 Trunk road (Taynuilt) (30mph Speed Limit) Order 2007
The Business Improvement Districts (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend the Business Improvement Districts (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (“the 2007 Regulations”). Schedule 2 of the 2007 Regulations sets out rules and procedures for holding business improvement district ballots, renewal ballots, and alteration ballots.

items.title: Act of Adjournal (Criminal Procedure Rules Amendment No. 6) (Criminal Proceedings etc. (Reform) (Scotland) Act 2007) 2007
items.description: This Act of Adjournal amends the Act of Adjournal (Criminal Procedure Rules) 1996 in consequence of the Criminal Proceedings etc. (Reform) (Scotland) Act 2007 (asp 6).

items.title: The Water Environment and Water Services (Scotland) Act 2003 (Commencement No. 6) Order 2007
items.description: This Order brings into force further provisions of the Water Environment and Water Services (Scotland) Act 2003 (“the 2003 Act”) on 30th November 2007.

items.title: The Licensing (Relevant Offences) (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: Section 129(1) of the Licensing (Scotland) Act 2005 (“the Act”) provides power to prescribe by regulations what offences are to count as a “relevant offence” in that Act. That phrase is used in a number of places in the Act – for example, section 21(3) and (4) requires the appropriate chief constable to notify a Licensing Board whether a person applying for a premises licence has or has not been convicted of any relevant offence.

items.title: The Local Government Pension Scheme (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Government Pension Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 1998 (“the 1998 Regulations”) and the Local Government Pension Scheme (Management and Investment of Funds) (Scotland) Regulations 1998 (“the Investment Regulations”).

items.title: The Public Health (Ships) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend the Public Health (Ships) (Scotland) Regulations 1971 (S.I. 1971/132, as previously amended by S.I. 1974/1008 and S.I. 1978/369) (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for public health control of ships arriving at or leaving ports in Scotland.

items.title: The Transport and Works (Scotland) Act 2007 (Commencement) Order 2007
items.description: This Order appoints 28th December 2007 for the coming into force of the provisions of the Transport and Works (Scotland) Act 2007 so far as not then in force (article 2).

items.title: The Transport and Works (Scotland) Act 2007 (Consequential and Transitional Provisions) Order 2007
items.description: This Order makes consequential modifications and transitional provision in connection with the coming into force of provisions of the Transport and Works (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Foot-and-Mouth Disease (Export and Movement Restrictions) (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations revoke and re-make with amendments the Import and Export Restrictions (Foot-and-Mouth Disease) (Scotland) (No. 6) Regulations 2007.

items.title: The Animal Welfare Act 2006 (Commencement No. 1) (Scotland) Order 2007
items.description: This Order brings into force, on 12th December 2007, sections 46 to 50 of the Animal Welfare Act 2006 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Registration Services (Miscellaneous Provisions) (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend the Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Miscellaneous Provisions) (Scotland) Regulations 1965 (“the 1965 Regulations”) to make amendments which are consequential on the Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Re-registration (Scotland) Regulations 2007 which allow district registrars to re-register births in certain circumstances (regulation 2(3)).

items.title: The Seed Potatoes (Fees) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st January 2008, amend the Seed Potatoes (Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 2005 (“the 2005 Regulations”), which set fees payable to the Scottish Ministers for certain matters arising under the Seed Potatoes (Scotland) Regulations 2000.

items.title: The Zootechnical Standards Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Zootechnical Standards Regulations 1992 (“the 1992 Regulations”).

items.title: The Official Feed and Food Controls (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations revoke and re-enact with changes the Official Feed and Food Controls (Scotland) Regulations (S.S.I. 2005/616). They extend to Scotland only. The Regulations provide for the execution and enforcement of Regulation (EC) No. 882/2004 of the European Parliament and of the Council on official controls performed to ensure the verification of compliance with feed and food law, animal health and animal welfare rules (O.J. No. L 165, 30.4.04, p.1), as last amended by Council Regulation (EC) No. 1791/2006 (O.J. No. L 363, 20.12.06, p.1) in relation to  “relevant feed law” and  “relevant food law”, which terms are defined in Schedules 2 and 3 respectively. The revised text of Regulation (EC) No. 882/2004 is now set out in a Corrigendum (O.J. No. L 191, 28.5.04, p.1), which should be read with a further Corrigendum (O.J. No. L 204, 4.8.07, p.29).

items.title: The Pesticides (Maximum Residue Levels in Crops, Food and Feeding Stuffs) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 4) Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which are made under section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972, amend the Pesticides (Maximum Residue Levels in Crops, Food and Feeding Stuffs) (Scotland) Regulations 2005 (S.S.I. 2005/599) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Fundable Bodies (Scotland) (No. 2) Order 2007
items.description: The Further and Higher Education (Scotland) Act 2005 (“the Act”) established the Scottish Further and Higher Education Funding Council and made provision relating to the funding of bodies which provide further and higher education. Those bodies are described in the Act as “fundable bodies” and are listed in schedule 2 to the Act.

items.title: The Protection of Charities Assets (Exemption) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2007
items.description: The Protection of Charities Assets (Exemption) (Scotland) Order 2006 (“the principal Order”) disapplied section 19(1) to (7) of the Charities and Trustee Investment (Scotland) Act 2005 in relation to the property owned by the bodies listed in the Schedule to the principal Order.

items.title: The A77 Trunk Road (Dalrymple Street, Girvan) (Special Event) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2007
The Criminal Proceedings etc. (Reform) (Scotland) Act 2007 (Commencement No. 2 and Transitional Provisions and Savings) Amendment Order 2007
items.description: This Order amends the Criminal Proceedings etc. (Reform) (Scotland) Act 2007 (Commencement No. 2 and Transitional Provisions and Savings) Order 2007 (“the Order”).

items.title: The Police (Promotion) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police (Promotion) (Scotland) Regulations 1996 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Water Environment (Drinking Water Protected Areas) (Scotland) Order 2007 (revoked)
items.description: This Order identifies those bodies of water used for the abstraction of drinking water, as required by section 6(1) of the Water Environment and Water Services (Scotland) Act 2003 (“the Act”); in doing so it implements the Scottish Ministers' obligation to identify such bodies of water as set out in paragraph 1 of Article 7 of Directive 2000/60/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council establishing a framework for Community action in the field of water policy (O.J. No. L 327, 22.12.2000, p.1).

items.title: The Registration of Civil Partnerships (Prescription of Forms, Publicisation and Errors) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend the arrangements for the form of the Register of Dissolutions of Civil Partnership held under section 122 of the Civil Partnership Act 2004. They provide that entries in that register held in the form of numbered extract Court of Session or sheriff court decrees may comprise copies of such extracts received in electronic form from the court.

items.title: The Water Environment and Water Services (Scotland) Act 2003 (Commencement No. 7) Order 2007
items.description: This Order brings into force further provisions of the Water Environment and Water Services (Scotland) Act 2003 (“the Act”) on 14th December 2007, which provide as follows:–

items.title: The Registration Services (Fees, etc.) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend the fees payable to the Registrar General for Scotland for services provided under the Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Scotland) Act 1965 (“the 1965 Act”), the Civil Partnership Act 2004 and the Local Electoral Administration and Registration Services (Scotland) Act 2006 (“the 2006 Act”).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms) 2007
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Table of Fees in Schedule 1 to the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers at Arms) (No. 2) 2002, by increasing the fees payable to messengers at arms by 3.6% with effect from 1st January 2008.

items.title: The Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003 (Conservation Bodies) Amendment Order 2007
items.description: This Order amends the Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003 (Conservation Bodies) Order 2003 by adding Glasgow City Heritage Trust and Stirling City Heritage Trust to the list of prescribed conservation bodies. Conservation bodies are bodies in favour of whom it is competent to create a conservation burden under section 38(1) of the Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003.

items.title: The Food Labelling (Declaration of Allergens) (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations extend to Scotland only. They amend the Food Labelling Regulations 1996 (“the principal Regulations”) which extend to the whole of Great Britain.

items.title: The Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003 (Rural Housing Bodies) Amendment (No. 2) Order 2007
items.description: This Order amends the Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003 (Rural Housing Bodies) Order 2004 by adding four new bodies to the list of prescribed rural housing bodies. Rural housing bodies are bodies  in favour of whom it is competent to create a rural housing burden comprising a right of pre-emption under section 43(1) of the Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003.

items.title: The Seeds (Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations prescribe fees–

items.title: The Fishery Products (Official Controls Charges) (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations extend to Scotland only. They revoke and replace the Fishery Products (Official Controls Charges) (Scotland) Regulations 2006 (S.S.I. 2006/579).

items.title: The Meat (Official Controls Charges) (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations extend to Scotland only. They revoke and replace the Meat (Official Controls Charges) (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (S.S.I. 2007/144).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Lands Valuation Appeal Court) 2007
items.description: This Act of Sederunt appoints the judges who may hear appeals under section 7 of the Valuation of Lands (Scotland) Amendment Act 1879. It revokes the Act of Sederunt (Lands Valuation Appeal Court) 2002, which previously made such provision.

items.title: The Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Re registration) (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations prescribe classes of case in which district registrars may re-register births under section 20 of the Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Scotland) Act 1965 (“the 1965 Act”) without authorisation by the Registrar General for Scotland (regulation 2).

items.title: The Criminal Proceedings etc. (Reform) (Scotland) Act 2007 (Incidental, Supplemental and Consequential Provisions) Order 2007
items.description: This Order makes amendments to the Criminal Proceedings etc. (Reform) (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the 2007 Act”) which are ancillary to the provisions of the 2007 Act.

items.title: The South West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 11) Order 2007
The South East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 11) Order 2007
The North West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 11) Order 2007
The North East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 11) Order 2007
The Licensing (Vessels etc.) (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: Section 126(10) of the Licensing (Scotland) Act 2005 (“the Act”) provides power to modify the operation of that statute in the case of vessels, vehicles and other moveable structures. Subject to such modifications and certain specific exemptions in the Act, the licensing regime created by the Act is to apply in its entirety to vessels, vehicles and moveable structures on or from which alcohol is sold.

items.title: The Licensing (Mandatory Conditions No. 2) (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: Schedule 3 to the Licensing (Scotland) Act 2005 (“the Act”) lists mandatory conditions which must be imposed by a Licensing Board on the granting of a premises licence. These Regulations amend that schedule in two respects.

items.title: The Glasgow City Council IFSD Tradeston Bridge Scheme 2007 Confirmation Instrument 2007
Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No. 10) (Miscellaneous) 2007
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes amendments to the Rules of the Court of Session 1994.

items.title: The Infant Formula and Follow-on Formula (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, give effect to Commission Directive 2006/141/EC on infant formulae and follow-on formulae and amending Directive 1999/21/EC (O.J. No. L 401, 30.12.06, p.1) and Council Directive 92/52/EEC on infant formulae and follow-on formulae intended for export to third countries (O.J. No. L 179, 1.7.92, p.129).

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Exempt Fireplaces) (Scotland) Order 2007
items.description: This Order, which extends to Scotland only, makes provision for exemption from the prohibition, under section 20 of the Clean Air Act 1993, on the emission of smoke in smoke control areas.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Sheriff Officers) 2007
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends Schedule 1 to the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Sheriff Officers) (No. 2) 2002 by making certain amendments of the general regulations and substituting a new Table of Fees.

items.title: The Budget (Scotland) Act 2007 Amendment Order 2007
items.description: This Order amends the Budget (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the 2007 Act”), which makes provision, for the financial year 2007/08, for the use of resources by the Scottish Administration and certain bodies whose expenditure is payable out of the Scottish Consolidated Fund, for authorising the use of resources, and for limits on the capital expenditure and borrowing of local authorities and certain other public bodies.

items.title: The Foot-and-Mouth Disease (Export and Movement Restrictions) (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description:
These Regulations revoke and re-make with amendments the Foot-and-Mouth Disease (Export and Movement Restrictions)

				(Scotland) Regulations 2007.

items.title: The Licensing (Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations make provision as to fees payable to Licensing Boards under the Licensing (Scotland) Act 2005. Provision is made for some fees to be determined by the Boards, subject to specified upper limits in certain cases and to the general rule on cost recovery in regulation 13. Other fees are of set amounts specified in the Regulations.

items.title: The Scottish Water (Loch Horn and Loch Lunndaidh) Water Order 2007
items.description: This version of the The Scottish Water (Loch Horn and Loch Lunndaidh) Water Order 2007 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A68 Trunk Road (Dalkeith Northern Bypass) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic, Temporary Prohibition of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restriction) (No. 2) Order 2007
The A68 Trunk Road (Dalkeith Northern Bypass) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic, Temporary Prohibition of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restriction) (No. 3) Order 2007
The Education (Recognised Bodies) (Scotland) Order 2007
items.description: This Order lists the name of all those bodies which appear to the Scottish Ministers to be recognised bodies for the purposes of section 214(2)(a) or (b) of the Education Reform Act 1988 (the “1988 Act”).

items.title: The Education (Listed Bodies) (Scotland) Order 2007
items.description: This Order lists the name of every body which is not a recognised body within section 216(4) of the Education Reform Act 1988 (the “1988” Act) but which appears to the Scottish Ministers either–

items.title: The Sheep and Goats (Identification and Traceability) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Sheep and Goats (Identification and Traceability) (Scotland) Regulations 2006 (“the principal Regulations”) to make provision for the administration and enforcement of Council Regulation (EC) No. 21/2004 (establishing a system for the identification and registration of ovine and caprine animals and amending Regulation (EC) No. 1782/2003 and Directives 92/102/EEC and 64/432/EEC) (“the Council Regulation”).

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Authorised Fuels) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, amend the Smoke Control Areas (Authorised Fuels) (Scotland) Regulations 2001.

items.title: The Inquiries (Scotland) Rules 2007
items.description: These Rules, made under section 41 of the Inquiries Act 2005 (“the Act”), deal with matters of evidence and procedure in relation to inquiries, the return or keeping, after the end of the inquiry, of documents given to or created by the inquiry and awards made by the chairman to the inquiry under section 40 of the Act (expenses of witnesses).

items.title: The A702 Trunk Road (Biggar High Street, Biggar) (Special Event) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2007
The Foot-and-Mouth Disease (Export Restrictions) (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations revoke and re-make with amendments the Foot-and-Mouth Disease (Export and Movement Restrictions) (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 2007.

items.title: The Scottish Police Services Authority (Police Support Services) (Modification) Order 2007
items.description: This Order modifies section 3 of the Police, Public Order and Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 2006 (asp 10) which defines the police support services which the Scottish Police Services Authority (“the Authority”) is under a duty to provide.

items.title: The Protection of Vulnerable Groups (Scotland) Act 2007 (Commencement No. 2) Order 2007
items.description: This Order brings into force certain provisions of the Protection of Vulnerable Groups (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Public Contracts and Utilities Contracts (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Public Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2006 (“the Public Contracts Regulations”) and the Utilities Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2006 (“the Utilities Contracts Regulations”).

items.title: The Local Electoral Administration and Registration Services (Scotland) Act 2006 (Commencement No. 3 and Transitional Provision) Order 2007
items.description: This Order brings into force on 14th January 2008 provisions of Part 2 of the Local Electoral Administration and Registration Services (Scotland) Act 2006 (“the 2006 Act”).

items.title: The Registration of Births, Still-births, Deaths and Marriages (Prescription of Forms) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations prescribe an application form for a third party to apply for notification of death and the form of notice that will be used by the Registrar General under the disclosure of death information scheme, as provided for in section 39B of the Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Scotland) Act 1965.

items.title: The Crofting Reform etc. Act 2007 (Commencement No. 2) Order 2007
items.description: This Order brings into force on 28th January 2008 all the provisions of the Crofting Reform etc. Act 2007 not already in force. These largely make amendments to the Crofters (Scotland) Act 1993 (“the 1993 Act”).

items.title: The Transport and Works (Scotland) Act 2007 (Consents under Enactments) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations make provision for–

items.title: The Legal Profession and Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 2007 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2007
items.description: This Order brings into force on 8th February 2007 sections 69(1), (2)(a) and (4) and 71, 73 and 76 of the Legal Profession and Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 2007.

items.title: The Transport and Works (Scotland) Act 2007 (Applications and Objections Procedure) Rules 2007
items.description: These Rules set out the procedures in connection with–

items.title: The Transport and Works (Scotland) Act 2007 (Inquiries and Hearings Procedure) Rules 2007
items.description: These Rules regulate the procedures prior to, the conduct of proceedings during and the procedures after, a public local inquiry or hearing held pursuant to section 9 of the Transport and Works (Scotland) Act 2007. These relate to applications or proposals for orders under section 1 of that Act to authorise transport systems and inland waterways.

items.title: The A83 Trunk Road (Inveraray) (Temporary Clearway) Order 2007
The Licensing (Transitional and Saving Provisions) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2007
items.description: This Order amends the Licensing (Transitional and Saving Provisions) (Scotland) Order 2007 (“2007 Order”) which makes provision in relation to the transition from the licensing regime under the Licensing (Scotland) Act 1976 (“1976 Act”) to the regime under the Licensing (Scotland) Act 2005 (“2005 Act”). For that purpose, the 2007 Order creates a “transitional period” running from 1st February 2008 to 1st September 2009.

items.title: The Gambling Act 2005 (Review of Premises Licences) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend the Gambling Act 2005 (Review of Premises Licences) (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Housing (Scotland) Act 2006 (Penalty Charge) Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations prescribe a penalty charge of £500 which is to be specified in notices issued under section 111 of the Housing (Scotland) Act 2006. Such a notice may be issued where there has been a breach of the duties under sections 98, 99(1), 101(2) or 103(2) of the Act to possess, and make available copies of certain prescribed documents when a house is to be put on the market for sale.

items.title: The Scottish Police Services Authority (Staff Transfer) (No. 2) Order 2007
items.description: This Order makes provision in relation to the transfer of staff to the Scottish Police Services Authority (“the Authority”) which is established under the Police, Public Order and Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 2006.

items.title: The Zoonoses and Animal By-Products (Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Poultry Breeding Flocks, Hatcheries and Animal By Products (Fees) (Scotland) Order 2002.

items.title: The North East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 12) Order 2007
The North West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 12) Order 2007
The Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003 (Rural Housing Bodies) Amendment Order 2007
items.description: This Order amends the Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003 (Rural Housing Bodies) Order 2004 by adding five bodies to the Schedule which lists bodies that are prescribed as rural housing bodies. A rural housing body is a body in favour of whom it is competent to create a rural housing burden comprising a right of pre emption under section 43(1) of the Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003. Section 43(6) provides that a body may be prescribed as a rural housing body only if it has as its object or function (or, as the case may be, one of its principal objects or functions) the provision of housing on rural land or the provision of such land for housing. The Scottish Ministers are satisfied that all five bodies referred to in the Schedule to this Order comply with this requirement.

items.title: The South East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 12) Order 2007
The South West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.12) Order 2007
The A9 Trunk Road (Highland Lights, Kessock Bridge) (Special Event) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2007
The A9 Trunk Road (Highland Lights, Kessock Bridge) (Special Event) (Temporary Restriction of Speed) Order 2007
The A7 Trunk Road (Auchenrivock Improvement) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic, Temporary Prohibition of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restriction) Order 2007
The Civil Legal Aid (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007
items.description: These Regulations amend the Civil Legal Aid (Scotland) Regulations 2002 as follows:–

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Ordinary Cause, Summary Application, Summary Cause and Small Claim Rules) Amendment (Miscellaneous) 2007
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes miscellaneous amendments to the rules of procedure in the sheriff court as follows.

items.title: The Advice and Assistance (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007
